title: 2-to-4 Binary Decoder (Logic Gate Implementation)
examTip: 2-to-4 Decoder: n inputs, 2^n outputs, exactly one output HIGH at a time. Truth table: AB=00→D0=1, AB=01→D1=1, AB=10→D2=1, AB=11→D3=1 (when EN=1). Decoders can implement any minterm (used in memory address decoding).
theory:
A Decoder is a combinational logic circuit that converts n binary input lines to 2^n unique output lines, where exactly one output is active (HIGH) at any given time corresponding to the binary value of the input. A 2-to-4 decoder has 2 input lines (A, B), an Enable (EN) input, and 4 output lines (D0–D3).

Operation: The EN (Enable) input acts as a master switch. When EN=0, all outputs are forced LOW regardless of A and B. When EN=1, exactly one output becomes HIGH based on the binary address AB. D0 = EN·A-bar·B-bar (active when AB=00), D1 = EN·A-bar·B (active when AB=01), D2 = EN·A·B-bar (active when AB=10), D3 = EN·A·B (active when AB=11).

Gate-Level Implementation: Two NOT gates generate A-bar and B-bar. Four 3-input AND gates each compute one output minterm: G_D0 = A-bar AND B-bar AND EN, G_D1 = A-bar AND B AND EN, G_D2 = A AND B-bar AND EN, G_D3 = A AND B AND EN. Note that inputs A and B are both fed (directly and through NOT gates) to multiple AND gates — hence the junction points in the wiring.

Decoder vs. Demultiplexer: A decoder has the EN pin always HIGH. A Demultiplexer (DEMUX) uses EN as a data input, routing that single data signal to one of 2^n outputs. Same hardware, different usage perspective.

Memory Address Decoding: The most critical use of decoders is in memory systems. A 10-bit address bus (A0–A9) can uniquely select one of 1024 memory locations using a 10-to-1024 decoder. Only one memory cell is activated at a time, preventing bus conflicts.
keyPoints: 2-to-4 Decoder: 2 inputs → 4 outputs, exactly one output HIGH at any time (when EN=1)
EN=0 disables all outputs — used for cascading multiple decoders
Can implement any minterm: D0=m0, D1=m1, D2=m2, D3=m3 of a 2-variable function
Inverse of an encoder: decoder has fewer inputs than outputs
When used as DEMUX, EN becomes the data input and AB selects the output channel
Cascading: two 2-to-4 decoders + MSB → one 3-to-8 decoder
useCases: Memory address decoding in RAM and ROM systems to select individual memory chips
Seven-segment display decoder (BCD to 7-segment) for driving digit displays
Instruction decode stage in CPU designs to activate the correct functional unit
